Cinematic tour · 5412 Fallbrook Ave

A photo is a moment.
A film is the walkthrough.

More eyes on the listing means more showings — and more showings means a faster close. AI just made the film almost free to produce.

+6% sale price

Sells for more

Homes marketed with video have sold for around 6% more (Realtor.com, 2023). On a $1M listing, that’s roughly $60,000.

2× the views

Seen by more buyers

Listings with a video pull about double the views and more saves than photo-only listings — more eyes, more showings.

32% faster

Off the market sooner

Strong visuals help homes sell about a third faster — fewer weeks of carrying costs and fewer price cuts.

Sources: Realtor.com (2023), Zillow listing data, and real-estate photography industry studies. Figures are market averages, not a guarantee for any individual home.

1bd / 1ba

Set behind private gates, this reimagined single-story home offers a bright open-concept plan with vaulted ceilings, a designer kitchen with oversized waterfall island, and sliding doors to a covered patio and outdoor kitchen. An attached 1-bed / 1-bath ADU with full kitchen and laundry is ideal for guests, multigenerational living, or rental income — minutes from the Rams practice facility, Warner Center, Westfield Topanga, and top-rated schools.

Filmed & enhanced by Estate Cinema

Photo enrichment

Every photo, lit like it’s worth the ask.

Same room. Same truth. We never change what the home is — we give the shot the light and depth a pro camera would have caught. Drag to compare.

Original Enhanced
How it works

Three steps. You never touch a timeline.

STEP 1

Send the photos

Shoot on any phone, room by room, in the order you’d walk a buyer through. That’s the only thing we need.

STEP 2

We direct the film

Camera moves, color, and pacing — composed and reviewed by a human, not dumped out by a machine. Every cut is checked before you see it.

STEP 3

Delivered in 48 hours

The full walkthrough film plus your enhanced stills, formatted for the MLS, YouTube, and every social feed.

Packages

See it first. Pay when you approve it.

Start with a free sample of your own listing. Move up only when it’s earning you showings.

You never pay for something you haven’t seen. Paid films start with a $200 deposit that covers production; the balance is due only when you approve the final cut. If it isn’t right, you don’t pay the rest.
